era: 1990-2003. Grunge/Punk. E=7 -- fast, crude, anti-polish. Fremont. Amphetamine Reptile.

theory_nodes.concept: Amphetamine Reptile Records enters engine at degree 321. AmRep: Minneapolis noise-rock label (Tom Hazelmeyer), anti-Sub Pop aesthetic, Gas Huffer as Seattle chapter.
theory_nodes.description: AmRep first appearance. Noise-rock counterweight to Sub Pop mythology.

theory_nodes.concept: Tom Price U-Men lineage: proto-grunge guitar vocabulary developed 1981-1989, carried to Gas Huffer 1990. Direct pre-grunge underground to post-summit connection.
theory_nodes.description: U-Men to Gas Huffer: the pre-grunge vocabulary persists.

theory_nodes.concept: Fremont enters engine. First Fremont band. Eccentric neighborhood: Troll (1990), Center of the Universe, Solstice Parade. Cheaper than Capitol Hill, scrappier aesthetic.

cross_references.relationship: E=7 continues. Post-summit plateau. Seattle to Fremont.
E=7 holds. Fremont second entry. AmRep to Sub Pop. Two Fremont labels in two degrees.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 + E=7 = E=14. Multi-layer simultaneous stacking on both tracks. Three musicians, three species, three registers each. The layering pairing.

milestones: AMPHETAMINE REPTILE FIRST APPEARANCE IN ENGINE
